organization.organization_background: Utkrusht is a proof-of-skills marketplace that helps agile teams find talent with strong fundamental concepts with within days. We help aspiring professionals improve their profile with continuous improvement in their skills, documenting their journey and helping them fulfill their potential. We primarily build, conduct, proctor and disseminate proof-of-skill assessments in Technical domains like AI, ML, Fullstack development, High scale distributed systems, DevOps etc but do help non-tech roles like Sales, HR, Marketing etc

role_context: At the BASIC proficiency level, a Python-Django developer is expected to build and maintain straightforward backend features in existing applications, such as creating or updating models, views, templates, forms, and simple API endpoints under established project patterns. They typically handle well-defined tasks like fixing defects, implementing small enhancements, writing basic database queries, and supporting integrations with guidance from more experienced engineers when requirements, architecture, or debugging become complex. They are expected to understand core Django concepts including URL routing, ORM usage, request-response flow, admin configuration, and migrations, and to apply them correctly in routine development work. Their judgment is focused on choosing standard framework-supported solutions, following coding conventions, and recognizing when performance issues, security concerns, or design decisions should be escalated to senior team members.
questions_prompt:
Please ensure the questions you ask cover the key functional, logical, architectural, implementation, debugging, optimization, review, and problem-solving areas from the competency scope for {name}, matched to {proficiency} proficiency ({yoe} years experience).

Use Python - Django real-world work-item scenarios. No additional competency long-scope topic areas or sub-skills were provided beyond the competency name itself, so focus on practical Python-Django application work such as implementing or fixing request/response flows, models and data-handling logic, views, forms, serializers if relevant to the scenario, URL routing, template or API behavior, authentication/authorization logic, database interaction patterns, code review, debugging, performance-minded improvements, and maintainability-oriented refactoring — while avoiding questions that depend on testing frameworks, test configuration, tool installation/setup, language or library syntax recall, or pure semantics.

This is a basic proficiency, so the tasks need to match one year of experience only. Somebody with one year of experience does not have extensive production experience and their concepts are usually shaky, so the complexity of the task needs to be basic only. Let the candidate be able to finish this within 15 minutes.

Prefer tasks that assess:
- understanding of requirements and constraints
- reasoning about ambiguous real-world situations
- quality of technical decisions and tradeoff analysis
- identifying root causes, risks, and edge cases
- proposing or implementing pragmatic solutions
- reviewing and improving existing work

Make the questions scenario-based and grounded in realistic day-to-day Django development work. Good task types include fixing a small broken feature, implementing a straightforward endpoint or page behavior, reviewing a short code snippet for bugs or design issues, identifying why a Django flow is failing, improving basic query/data-access behavior, handling validation or permissions correctly, or making a simple maintainability improvement. Avoid trivia, gotchas, memorization-heavy prompts, and anything requiring obscure framework internals.

The goal is to evaluate applied competence, practical problem-solving, implementation and review ability, sound technical judgment, and the candidate's ability to deliver realistic solutions within the expected time constraints for their proficiency level.
